results.question: How many senior compliance professionals will we recruit by end of Q3 2026?
results.answer:
We plan to recruit 12 senior regulatory and compliance professionals by the end of Q3 2026. This information is stated in our board strategy memo for Q2 2026 [Doc: board_strategy_memo_q2_2026.pdf, p.1]. 

* Number of senior compliance professionals to be recruited by end of Q3 2026: 12 [Doc: board_strategy_memo_q2_2026.pdf, p.1]
* Average number of days senior compliance roles remain unfilled: 97 days [Doc: board_strategy_memo_q2_2026.pdf, p.2]

results.question: What was the YoY growth in digital asset licenses from Q2 2025 to Q2 2026?
results.answer:
No relevant information found on the year-over-year growth in digital asset licenses from Q2 2025 to Q2 2026. However, here are some related trends in digital assets [Web: fidelitydigitalassets.com]:
* Continued integration of digital assets into the broader financial system
* Increased focus on token holder rights and on-chain businesses prioritizing mechanisms such as buybacks and clearer ownership rights
* Institutional momentum and macro uncertainty shaping the digital asset market
* Potential for a breakout and return to new all-time highs in 2026 due to shifting liquidity conditions and policy signals.

results.question: Which initiatives are flagged "At Risk"?
results.answer:
Two initiatives are currently flagged as 'At Risk'. 
* Digital Asset Licensing Fast-Track (Regulatory Affairs): Consultant delivery delayed by 3 weeks. Revised SLA from legal team still outstanding. Risk of missing the Q3 2026 go-live target which would cede first-mover advantage to DIFC [Doc: initiative_status_report_q2_2026.pdf, p.1].
* Talent Attraction Programme (HR): 40% complete [Doc: initiative_status_report_q2_2026.pdf, p.1].

results.answer:
The deadline for the AML/CFT Framework Update (FATF) is August 15, 2026.

results.question: Which initiatives are at risk or behind schedule?
results.answer:
Two initiatives are currently flagged as 'At Risk' and two as 'Behind Schedule'. 
The initiatives at risk or behind schedule are:
* Digital Asset Licensing Fast-Track (At Risk): Consultant delivery delayed by 3 weeks. Revised SLA from legal team still outstanding. Risk of missing the Q3 2026 go-live target which would cede first-mover advantage to DIFC [Doc: initiative_status_report_q2_2026.pdf, p.1].
* Talent Attraction Programme (At Risk): Visa processing delays averaging 47 days are deterring senior international candidates. Escalation to immigration authority required at ministerial level [Doc: initiative_status_report_q2_2026.pdf, p.1].
* CSO Intelligence Dashboard (Behind): Technology vendor change mid-project caused a 6-week delay. New delivery date: 15 September 2026. Contingency: manual reporting continues [Doc: initiative_status_report_q2_2026.pdf, p.1].
* Core Registry Upgrade (Behind): Procurement process extended due to additional security review requirements. Revised kick-off: 1 August 2026 [Doc: initiative_status_report_q2_2026.pdf, p.1].

results.question: What milestones were completed in Q2 2026?
results.answer:
Here are the milestones completed in Q2 2026:
 
The following milestones were completed in Q2 2026. These achievements reflect progress on strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing our financial center's capabilities and competitiveness.
 
* Financial Innovation Hub: Three anchor tenants signed (global payments firm, regtech startup, Islamic fintech) [Doc: initiative_status_report_q2_2026.pdf, p.1]
* ESG Reporting Framework: Draft framework circulated to 45 licensed entities; 38 responses received [Doc: initiative_status_report_q2_2026.pdf, p.1]
* GCC Bilateral Agreements: MOU signed with Bahrain Economic Development Board; discussions advanced with Kuwait CMA [Doc: initiative_status_report_q2_2026.pdf, p.1]
